name: Amniotic fluid volume at peak
tier_rationale: Tier A from Brace & Wolf 1989: AFV rises through mid-pregnancy to ~800 mL peak, falls toward term. Well-replicated reference trajectory.

name: Gestational week of peak amniotic fluid volume
tier_rationale: Tier A from Brace & Wolf 1989: AFV peak at 32-34 weeks; fetal-urine production and swallowing approach steady state.

name: Amniotic fluid volume at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from Brace & Wolf 1989: AFV declines from peak to ~600 mL at term; oligohydramnios threshold conventionally AFI<5 cm or single deepest pocket<2 cm.

name: Amniotic fluid volume at 20 weeks
tier_rationale: Tier A from Brace & Wolf 1989: AFV at 20 weeks ~350 mL; interpolated reference point on the trajectory.

name: Amniotic fluid volume early-pregnancy baseline
tier_rationale: Tier B from Brace & Wolf 1989: early-pregnancy (~12-14 weeks) AFV is ~50-200 mL; serves as the baseline anchor for the Gaussian-bump trajectory model. Wide CI reflects sparser early-pregnancy data.
notes: Used as the baseline offset in the amniotic_fluid_volume_trajectory submodel (Gaussian-bump approximation to the Brace & Wolf 1989 curve).

name: Amniotic fluid volume Gaussian-bump spread (sigma)
tier_rationale: Tier C: empirical Gaussian-fit spread parameter. Approximates the Brace & Wolf 1989 curve shape; the true curve is closer to a piecewise polynomial. The Gaussian approximation is documented in the submodel's reference kernel.
notes: Single-fit value, not from a meta-analysis. The downstream submodel inherits Tier C from this parameter.
